column18: On average, each warp of this workload spends 1.5 cycles being stalled waiting on a fixed latency execution dependency. Typically, this stall reason should be very low and only shows up as a top contributor in already highly optimized kernels. Try to hide the corresponding instruction latencies by increasing the number of active warps, restructuring the code or unrolling loops. Furthermore, consider switching to lower-latency instructions, e.g. by making use of fast math compiler options. This stall type represents about 35.5% of the total average of 4.3 cycles between issuing two instructions.
